1: Why Why hidest thou thyself thyself in times of trouble?
2: Let them be taken in the devices that they have imagined.
3: And blesseth the covetous, whom whom the LORD LORD abhorreth.
4: God is is not in all his thoughts.
5: As for As for all his enemies, he puffeth at them.
6: For I shall I shall never be be in adversity.
7: Under his tongue is is mischief and vanity.
8: In the secret places doth he murder the innocent: His eyes are privily set against the poor.
9: He doth catch the poor, when he draweth him into his net.
10: That the poor may fall by his strong ones.
11: He hideth his face; he will never see it it .
13: He hath said in his heart, Thou wilt not require it it .
14: The poor committeth himself unto thee; Thou art the helper of the fatherless.
15: And the evil man: man: seek out his wickedness till till thou find none.
16: The heathen are perished out of his land.
17: Thou wilt prepare their heart, thou wilt cause thine ear to hear:
18: That the man of the earth may no more oppress.
